architecture.summary: A survey of Retrieval And Structuring (RAS) Augmented Generation methods that integrate dynamic information retrieval (sparse, dense, and hybrid approaches) with structured knowledge representations (taxonomy construction, hierarchical classification, information extraction) to augment LLMs. Information flows from external retrieval engines into structured intermediate representations, which are then integrated with LLMs via prompt-based methods, reasoning frameworks, and knowledge embedding techniques to mitigate hallucination, outdated knowledge, and domain expertise limitations.

biblio.title: Retrieval And Structuring Augmented Generation with Large Language Models

claims.statement: LLMs face critical challenges when deployed in real-world applications, including hallucination generation, outdated knowledge, and limited domain expertise
Retrieval And Structuring Augmented Generation addresses limitations by integrating dynamic information retrieval with structured knowledge representations
Retrieval mechanisms include sparse, dense, and hybrid approaches for accessing external knowledge
Text structuring techniques such as taxonomy construction, hierarchical classification, and information extraction transform unstructured text into organized representations
Structured representations integrate with LLMs through prompt-based methods, reasoning frameworks, and knowledge embedding techniques
Technical challenges include retrieval efficiency, structure quality, and knowledge integration
